name: Lumen
short_description: An interactive tabletop display whose grid of light guides rises and changes color via shape-memory-alloy actuators, presenting calm, organic moving shapes for ambient computing.
long_description: Lumen, presented at SIGGRAPH 2004 Emerging Technologies by Ivan Poupyrev and colleagues at Sony, is an electromechanical display made of a two-dimensional array of movable light guides whose height and color can be controlled independently. Each guide is raised and lowered by a shape-memory-alloy (SMA) string that contracts when heated by current, producing smooth, noiseless, organic motion, while a capacitive SmartSkin sensor embedded in the surface recognizes hand shapes and finger motions for direct touch interaction. As an early shape-changing / actuated tangible display, it matters to physical AI and interaction design because it treats physical form itself as an expressive, ambient information medium rather than a screen, foreshadowing later shape displays and 'calm computing' surfaces.
ai_note: Input is capacitive SmartSkin touch sensing and SMA actuation; the paper explicitly contrasts this with camera techniques, and no machine learning is used.

images.caption: A hand interacting with the Lumen display, whose grid of light guides rises to form an illuminated shape.
images.alt_text: A hand touching a small square tabletop display whose array of glowing pixel rods rises at different heights to form a raised, illuminated shape.

sources.title: Lumen: interactive visual and shape display for calm computing
Lumen: Interactive Visual and Shape Display for Calm Computing (Emerging Technologies entry)
Lumen: Interactive Visual and Shape Display for Calm Computing (paper PDF)
sources.publisher: Sony CSL Interaction Lab / ACM SIGGRAPH

interactions.title: Sculpt light and shape with your hand
interactions.description: Place a hand on the surface and the capacitive SmartSkin sensor reads your hand shape and finger motion, raising and coloring the grid of light guides under your touch so you form glowing shapes and images directly with your hands.

interactions.title: Feel a picture by touch
interactions.description: Because every pixel is a physical rod, an image like a fish or water flowing from a tap is rendered as a touchable relief you can actively explore with a moving hand, with no glove or stylus in between — the shape is felt directly.
interactions.source_label: Poupyrev et al., TEI 2007 (Actuation and TUIs)

interactions.title: A character that touches back
interactions.description: In a demonstrated scenario, rest your hand on the surface and a virtual fish swims over and taps it with its nose, or a bouncing ball rebounds off your palm so you feel the impact — the figure on screen reaches back out into the physical surface.
interactions.source_label: Poupyrev et al., TEI 2007 (Actuation and TUIs)

interactions.title: Buttons that rise only when you need them
interactions.description: The team built pressable tangible buttons that the display raises on demand and lowers away when unused, so physical controls appear out of a flat surface and reshape themselves to match the current state and function.
interactions.source_label: Poupyrev et al., TEI 2007 (Actuation and TUIs)

interactions.title: Motion as an extra layer of meaning
interactions.description: A single graphic can be overlaid with different physical motion to change its meaning: a play triangle with a slow wave passing through it reads as playback, a fast wave as fast-forward, and a reversed wave as rewind, so shape encodes the state the icon alone cannot.
interactions.source_label: Poupyrev et al., TEI 2007 (Actuation and TUIs)

interactions.title: Touch someone over the network
interactions.description: Linking two Lumen units over a network creates a haptic communication link where two distant people can touch and draw simple traces on each other's hand — your own graphics give feedback on your input while the remote person's actions arrive as moving physical shape.
interactions.source_label: Poupyrev et al., TEI 2007 (Actuation and TUIs)

interactions.title: A wall that breathes the weather
interactions.description: Scaled up and embedded in a wall or ceiling as the authors envisioned, a calm tile could let through a slow rolling swell of raised, tinted rods when rain is coming and lie flat and warm-lit on a clear day, read at a glance with no screen to attend to — grounded in Lumen's silent SMA actuation and per-pixel color and height control.

interactions.title: A goodnight squeeze across distance
interactions.description: Extending the demonstrated remote-touch link, a child and a traveling parent could each press a palm to their own tile so a slow pulse rises under the other's hand as a wordless goodnight — built only on the paired-device haptic touch-and-trace already shown.
